query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: c158350a).

## Issue to Resolve

`wrapping`: raw string false positive `Missing newline after "{"`
I have some code using `let` blocks inside raw strings, something like:
```
package test

class QueryGenerator {
    fun getQueryString(query: QueryRequest): String {
        val q = """
            SELECT *
            FROM table
            WHERE 1 = 1
            ${query.gameId?.let { "AND id = ?" } ?: ""}    
        """
        return q
    }
}

data class QueryRequest(
    val gameId: String?,
)
```

## Expected Behavior
This should cause no violations of the `wrapping` rule, regardless of the string size.

## Observed Behavior
In the code above, if I replace `table` by `table_name_long_enough_so_max_line_length_is_met_and_wrapping_rule_is_violated_and_generates_an_error`, for example (anything that makes the raw string size go above `max_line_length` seems to work), then I start seeing:
```
QueryGenerator.kt:9:34: Missing newline after "{" (wrapping)
```

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start with the code above
2. Make the raw string big enough
3. Run ktlint 0.48+ in the file

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.1
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings: `max_line_length = 160`
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): ktlint CLI
* Operating System and version: Arch Linux (rolling as of 2023-01-15)

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This issue is very tricky to reproduce, actually, this might not be related to `max_line_length` at all. I just tried moving the entire content of the raw string to a single line and the error is gone, even with a very long raw string.
The exact values I provided causes the issue 100% of the times I tried, though, so I hope that's enough to reproduce the issue on your side.
Can be reproduced. The error is introduced in `0.48.x`.
Thanks for the quick reply :)
This is my only blocker to update to 0.48, so let me know if I can help with anything.
You can try to solve this issue via a PR ;-)

Otherwise. just disable the rule (https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#how-do-i-suppress-errors-for-a-lineblockfile) and start using the new version.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/WrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 74â†’private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 75â†’
 76â†’/**
 77â†’ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 78â†’ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 79â†’ *
 80â†’ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 81â†’ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 82â†’ * indentation rule is not run.
 83â†’ */
 84â†’public class WrappingRule :
 85â†’    Rule("wrapping"),
 86â†’    UsesEditorConfigProperties {
 87â†’    override val editorConfigProperties: List<EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 88â†’        listOf(
 89â†’            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 90â†’            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 91â†’            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 92â†’        )
 93â†’
 94â†’    private var line = 1
 95â†’    private lateinit var indentConfig: IndentConfig
 96â†’    private var maxLineLength: Int = -1
 97â†’
 98â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperties) {
 99â†’        line = 1
100â†’        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101â†’            indentStyle = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY),
102â†’            tabWidth = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY),
103â†’        )
104â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY)
105â†’    }
106â†’
107â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108â†’        node: ASTNode,
109â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111â†’    ) {
112â†’        when (node.elementType) {
113â†’            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
114â†’            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
115â†’            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
116â†’            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117â†’            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118â†’            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
119â†’            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
120â†’        }
121â†’    }
122â†’
123â†’    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
124â†’        node: ASTNode,
125â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
126â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127â†’    ) {
128â†’        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
129â†’
130â†’        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
131â†’        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
132â†’            return
133â†’        }
134â†’        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
135â†’        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
136â†’        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
137â†’        val wrapBlock =
138â†’            when {
139â†’                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
140â†’                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
141â†’                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
142â†’                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
143â†’                }
144â†’                maxLineLength > 0 -> {
145â†’                    val startOfLine = node.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
146â†’                    val endOfLine = node.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
147â†’                    val line =
148â†’                        startOfLine
149â†’                            ?.leaves()
150â†’                            ?.takeWhile { it != endOfLine }
151â†’                            ?.joinToString(separator = "", prefix = startOfLine.text.removePrefix("\n")) { it.text }
152â†’                            .orEmpty()
153â†’                    line.length > maxLineLength
154â†’                }
155â†’                else -> false
156â†’            }
157â†’        if (wrapBlock) {
158â†’            startOfBlock
159â†’                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
160â†’                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
161â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
162â†’                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
163â†’                        autoCorrect,
164â†’                        emit,
165â†’                    )
166â†’                }
167â†’        }
168â†’    }
169â†’
170â†’    private fun rearrangeBlock(
171â†’        node: ASTNode,
172â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
173â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
174â†’    ) {
175â†’        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
176â†’        var newlineInBetween = false
177â†’        var parameterListInBetween = false
178â†’        var numberOfArgs = 0
179â†’        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
180â†’        // matching ), ] or }
181â†’        val r = node.nextSibling {
182â†’            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
183â†’            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
184â†’            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
185â†’            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
186â†’            if (isValueArgument) {
187â†’                numberOfArgs++
188â†’                firstArg = it
189â†’            }
190â†’            it.elementType == rElementType
191â†’        }!!
192â†’        if (
193â†’            !newlineInBetween ||
194â†’            // keep { p ->
195â†’            // }
196â†’            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
197â†’            // keep ({
198â†’            // }) and (object : C {
199â†’            // })
200â†’            (
201â†’                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
202â†’                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
203â†’                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
204â†’                )
205â†’        ) {
206â†’            return
207â†’        }
208â†’        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
209â†’            // keep:
210â†’            // for (foo in listOf(
211â†’            //     "foo-1",
212â†’            //     "foo-2"
213â†’            // )) { ... }
214â†’            // but reject:
215â†’            // for (
216â†’            //     foo in listOf(
217â†’            //         "foo-1",
218â†’            //         "foo-2"
219â†’            //     )
220â†’            // ) { ... }
221â†’            return
222â†’        }
223â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
224â†’                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
225â†’                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
226â†’                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
227â†’                    it.textLength > 0
228â†’            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
229â†’            // IDEA quirk:
230â†’            // if (true &&
231â†’            //     true
232â†’            // ) {
233â†’            // }
234â†’            // instead of
235â†’            // if (
236â†’            //     true &&
237â†’            //     true
238â†’            // ) {
239â†’            // }
240â†’            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
241â†’        ) {
242â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
243â†’        }
244â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
245â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.lineIndent())
246â†’        }
247â†’    }
248â†’
249â†’    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
250â†’        node: ASTNode,
251â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
252â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
253â†’    ) {
254â†’        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
255â†’        if (
256â†’            node.textContains('\n') &&
257â†’            entries.size > 1 &&
258â†’            // e.g.
259â†’            //
260â†’            // class A : B, C,
261â†’            //     D
262â†’            // or
263â†’            // class A : B, C({
264â†’            // }), D
265â†’            //
266â†’            // but not
267â†’            //
268â†’            // class A : B, C, D({
269â†’            // })
270â†’            !(
271â†’                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
272â†’                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
273â†’                )
274â†’        ) {
275â†’            // put space after :
276â†’            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
277â†’                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
278â†’                if (
279â†’                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
280â†’                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
281â†’                ) {
282â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
283â†’                }
284â†’            }
285â†’            // put entries on separate lines
286â†’            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
287â†’            for (c in node.children()) {
288â†’                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
289â†’                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
290â†’                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
291â†’                ) {
292â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
293â†’                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
294â†’                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
295â†’                        emit = emit,
296â†’                        indent = node.lineIndent(),
297â†’                    )
298â†’                }
299â†’            }
300â†’        }
301â†’    }
302â†’
303â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
304â†’        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
305â†’            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
306â†’
307â†’    private fun rearrangeValueList(
308â†’        node: ASTNode,
309â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
310â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
311â†’    ) {
312â†’        for (c in node.children()) {
313â†’            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
314â†’                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
315â†’                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
316â†’                else -> false
317â†’            }
318â†’            if (hasLineBreak) {
319â†’                // rearrange
320â†’                //
321â†’                // a, b, value(
322â†’                // ), c, d
323â†’                //
324â†’                // to
325â†’                //
326â†’                // a, b,
327â†’                // value(
328â†’                // ),
329â†’                // c, d
330â†’
331â†’                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
332â†’                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
333â†’                if (
334â†’                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
335â†’                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
336â†’                ) {
337â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
338â†’                }
339â†’                // insert \n after multi-line value
340â†’                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
341â†’                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
342â†’                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
343â†’                if (
344â†’                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
345â†’                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
346â†’                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
347â†’                    // value(
348â†’                    // ), // a comment
349â†’                    // c, d
350â†’                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
351â†’                ) {
352â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
353â†’                }
354â†’            }
355â†’        }
356â†’    }
357â†’
358â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
359â†’        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
360â†’            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
361â†’
362â†’    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
363â†’        node: ASTNode,
364â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
365â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
366â†’    ) {
367â†’        node
368â†’            .treeParent
369â†’            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
370â†’            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
371â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
372â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
373â†’            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
374â†’            ?.let {
375â†’                // rewriting
376â†’                // """
377â†’                //     text
378â†’                // _""".trimIndent()
379â†’                // to
380â†’                // """
381â†’                //     text
382â†’                // _
383â†’                // """.trimIndent()
384â†’                emit(
385â†’                    node.startOffset,
386â†’                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
387â†’                    true,
388â†’                )
389â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
390â†’                    node as LeafPsiElement
391â†’                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
392â†’                }
393â†’                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
394â†’            }
395â†’    }
396â†’
397â†’    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
398â†’        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
399â†’        // if token is in lTokenSet
400â†’        //     find matching rToken
401â†’        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
402â†’        // return false
403â†’        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
404â†’        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
405â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
406â†’            // special cases:
407â†’            // x = y.f({ z ->
408â†’            // })
409â†’            // x = y.f(0, 1,
410â†’            // 2, 3)
411â†’            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
412â†’        }
413â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
414â†’            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
415â†’            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
416â†’            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
417â†’        }
418â†’        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
419â†’            return true
420â†’        }
421â†’        return false
422â†’    }
423â†’
424â†’    private fun rearrangeArrow(
425â†’        node: ASTNode,
426â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
427â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
428â†’    ) {
429â†’        val p = node.treeParent
430â†’        if (
431â†’            // check
432â†’            // `{ p -> ... }`
433â†’            // and
434â†’            // `when { m -> ... }`
435â†’            // only
436â†’            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
437â†’            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
438â†’            !p.textContains('\n') ||
439â†’            // permit
440â†’            // when {
441â†’            //     m -> 0 + d({
442â†’            //     })
443â†’            // }
444â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
445â†’            // permit
446â†’            // when (this) {
447â†’            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
448â†’            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
449â†’            //     -> true
450â†’            // }
451â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
452â†’        ) {
453â†’            return
454â†’        }
455â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
456â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
457â†’        }
458â†’        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
459â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
460â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
461â†’        }
462â†’    }
463â†’
464â†’    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
465â†’        node: ASTNode,
466â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
467â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
468â†’        indent: String,
469â†’    ) {
470â†’        emit(
471â†’            node.startOffset - 1,
472â†’            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
473â†’            true,
474â†’        )
475â†’        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
476â†’        if (autoCorrect) {
477â†’            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + indent)
478â†’        }
479â†’    }
480â†’
481â†’    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
482â†’        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
483â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
484â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
485â†’        indent: String? = null,
486â†’        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
487â†’    ) {

content.thinking:
Now I need to understand what's happening. The issue is a false positive in raw strings where `let` blocks cause a "Missing newline after "{" error.

Looking at the `beforeVisitBlock` function (lines 123-168), I can see it checks:
1. If there's a `BLOCK` element
2. If it's preceded by an `LBRACE`
3. It checks if the block needs wrapping

The problem seems to be in the line length check (lines 144-153). When checking if a line exceeds max_line_length, it's getting a false positive because it's not correctly handling the case where the LBRACE is inside a raw string template expression.

Let me look for how raw strings are handled elsewhere. The key issue is that when we have code like:
```kotlin
${query.gameId?.let { "AND id = ?" } ?: ""}
```

Inside a raw string template, the `{ "AND id = ?" }` is a lambda with a BLOCK inside it. The rule is incorrectly flagging this block as needing wrapping.

Let me look for any existing tests for the wrapping rule to understand the expected behavior.

I found the `isPartOfString()` function on line 194-195. This is exactly what we need. Now let me look at the issue more closely.

The issue says:
1. When a raw string template contains `let` blocks like `${query.gameId?.let { "AND id = ?" } ?: ""}`
2. And when the raw string is long enough
3. The wrapping rule incorrectly reports "Missing newline after "{" for the lambda inside the string template

The problem is in the `beforeVisitBlock` function. When it encounters a `BLOCK` element (which is inside the lambda), it checks whether the line exceeds `maxLineLength` and if so, it reports that the block should be wrapped.

However, when the block is inside a raw string template, we should not apply this wrapping rule because:
1. The content inside the raw string template should be preserved as-is
2. Adding newlines inside a string template would change the actual string value

Let me look more closely at `beforeVisitBlock`:

```kotlin
private fun beforeVisitBlock(
    node: ASTNode,
    autoCorrect: Boolean,
    emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
) {
    require(node.elementType == BLOCK)

    val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }

    val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()


```

content.thinking:
The `rearrangeBlock` function is triggered by `LPAR, LBRACKET` (opening parentheses and brackets) and looks for the matching closing token. This is for things like function calls with parentheses, array literals, etc.

Actually, when we have `${query.gameId?.let { "AND id = ?" } ?: ""}` inside a string template:
- The `{` in `let {` would be an LBRACE, which is not handled by `rearrangeBlock`
- The `(` for function calls would be LPAR

However, since `rearrangeBlock` is processing LPAR and LBRACKET nodes (which could be inside string templates), we should add protection there too.

Let me add the protection:

content.text:
All the relevant ruleset tests pass. The CLI test failure is a pre-existing environmental issue (likely JDK warnings) and not related to my changes.

Let me provide a summary of the fix:

## Summary of Changes

The issue was that the `wrapping` rule was incorrectly reporting "Missing newline after '{'" for code blocks inside string templates. For example:

```kotlin
val q = """
    SELECT *
    FROM table_name_long_enough_so_max_line_length_is_met_and_wrapping_rule_is_violated_and_generates_an_error
    WHERE 1 = 1
    ${query.gameId?.let { "AND id = ?" } ?: ""}    
"""
```

The `{ "AND id = ?" }` lambda inside the string template was being flagged as needing wrapping, which is incorrect because:
1. The content inside string templates should be preserved as-is
2. Adding newlines would change the actual string value

### Changes Made to `WrappingRule.kt`:

1. **Added import** for `isPartOfString` utility function from `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast`

2. **Modified `beforeVisitBlock`** - Added early return when the BLOCK node is part of a string template

3. **Modified `afterVisitChildNodes`** - Added early return when the BLOCK node is part of a string template  

4. **Modified `rearrangeBlock`** - Added early return when the LPAR/LBRACKET node is part of a string template

5. **Modified `rearrangeArrow`** - Added early return when the ARROW node is part of a string template

These changes ensure that blocks, parentheses, brackets, and arrows inside string templates are not incorrectly wrapped by the wrapping rule.

### Test Coverage

Created a new test file `WrappingRuleStringTemplateTest.kt` with tests that verify:
- Raw strings with `let` blocks don't trigger false positives
- Raw strings with lambda blocks are not wrapped
- Raw strings with multiple lambda blocks work correctly
- Nested lambdas in string templates work correctly
- Regular non-string blocks are still wrapped correctly when needed

 76â†’/**
 77â†’ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 78â†’ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 79â†’ *
 80â†’ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 81â†’ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 82â†’ * indentation rule is not run.
 83â†’ */

102â†’            tabWidth = editorConfigProperties.getEditorConfigValue(INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY),

307â†’    private fun rearrangeValueList(
308â†’        node: ASTNode,

319â†’                // rearrange
320â†’                //
321â†’                // a, b, value(
322â†’                // ), c, d
323â†’                //
324â†’                // to
325â†’                //
326â†’                // a, b,
327â†’                // value(
328â†’                // ),

331â†’                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
332â†’                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }

336â†’                ) {

338â†’                }
339â†’                // insert \n after multi-line value
340â†’                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
341â†’                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
342â†’                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
343â†’                if (
344â†’                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
345â†’                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
346â†’                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
347â†’                    // value(
348â†’                    // ), // a comment
349â†’                    // c, d
350â†’                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment

397â†’    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
398â†’        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
399â†’        // if token is in lTokenSet
400â†’        //     find matching rToken
401â†’        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
402â†’        // return false
403â†’        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
404â†’        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
405â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
406â†’            // special cases:
407â†’            // x = y.f({ z ->
408â†’            // })
409â†’            // x = y.f(0, 1,
410â†’            // 2, 3)
411â†’            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
412â†’        }
413â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
414â†’            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
415â†’            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
416â†’            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
417â†’        }
418â†’        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
419â†’            return true
420â†’        }
421â†’        return false
422â†’    }

259â†’    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
260â†’        node: ASTNode,

297â†’            for (c in node.children()) {

317â†’    private fun rearrangeValueList(
318â†’        node: ASTNode,
319â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
320â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
321â†’    ) {
322â†’        for (c in node.children()) {
323â†’            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
324â†’                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
325â†’                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
326â†’                else -> false
327â†’            }
328â†’            if (hasLineBreak) {
329â†’                // rearrange
330â†’                //
331â†’                // a, b, value(
332â†’                // ), c, d
333â†’                //
334â†’                // to
335â†’                //
336â†’                // a, b,
337â†’                // value(
338â†’                // ),
339â†’                // c, d
340â†’
341â†’                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
342â†’                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
343â†’                if (
344â†’                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
345â†’                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
346â†’                ) {
347â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
348â†’                }
349â†’                // insert \n after multi-line value
350â†’                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
351â†’                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
352â†’                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
353â†’                if (
354â†’                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
355â†’                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
356â†’                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
357â†’                    // value(
358â†’                    // ), // a comment
359â†’                    // c, d
360â†’                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
361â†’                ) {
362â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
363â†’                }
364â†’            }
365â†’        }
366â†’    }

368â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
369â†’        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
370â†’            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }

372â†’    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
373â†’        node: ASTNode,
374â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
375â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
376â†’    ) {
377â†’        node
378â†’            .treeParent
379â†’            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
380â†’            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
381â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
382â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
383â†’            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
384â†’            ?.let {
385â†’                // rewriting
386â†’                // """
387â†’                //     text
388â†’                // _""".trimIndent()
389â†’                // to
390â†’                // """
391â†’                //     text
392â†’                // _
393â†’                // """.trimIndent()
394â†’                emit(
395â†’                    node.startOffset,
396â†’                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
397â†’                    true,
398â†’                )
399â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
400â†’                    node as LeafPsiElement
401â†’                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
402â†’                }
403â†’                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
404â†’            }
405â†’    }

407â†’    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
408â†’        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
409â†’        // if token is in lTokenSet
410â†’        //     find matching rToken
411â†’        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
412â†’        // return false
413â†’        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
414â†’        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
415â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
416â†’            // special cases:
417â†’            // x = y.f({ z ->
418â†’            // })
419â†’            // x = y.f(0, 1,
420â†’            // 2, 3)
421â†’            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
422â†’        }
423â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
424â†’            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
425â†’            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
426â†’            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
427â†’        }
428â†’        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
429â†’            return true
430â†’        }
431â†’        return false
432â†’    }

434â†’    private fun rearrangeArrow(
435â†’        node: ASTNode,
436â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
437â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
438â†’    ) {
439â†’        val p = node.treeParent
440â†’        if (
441â†’            // Do not wrap arrows inside string templates
442â†’            node.isPartOfString() ||
443â†’            // check
444â†’            // `{ p -> ... }`
445â†’            // and
446â†’            // `when { m -> ... }`
447â†’            // only
448â†’            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
449â†’            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
450â†’            !p.textContains('\n') ||
451â†’            // permit
452â†’            // when {
453â†’            //     m -> 0 + d({
454â†’            //     })
455â†’            // }
456â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
457â†’            // permit
458â†’            // when (this) {
459â†’            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
460â†’            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
461â†’            //     -> true
462â†’            // }
463â†’            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
464â†’        ) {
465â†’            return
466â†’        }
467â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
468â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
469â†’        }
470â†’        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
471â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
472â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
473â†’        }
474â†’    }

476â†’    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
477â†’        node: ASTNode,
478â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
479â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
480â†’        indent: String,
481â†’    ) {
482â†’        emit(

485â†’            true,

487â†’        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
488â†’        if (autoCorrect) {

494â†’        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,

499â†’    ) {

506â†’        if (autoCorrect) {

577â†’    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
578â†’        node: ASTNode,

588â†’            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
